af_score_components.notes: Abaqus FEA (Finite Element Analysis) MCP server. Has MCP (quality 70). Wraps Dassault Systèmes CAD/simulation software. Documentation assumes Abaqus expertise (not agent-friendly). License management required. Complex operations are async. auth_complexity=80 for Abaqus license auth.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for network operations. Abaqus license authentication (credential required). CAD file handling requires careful access control (IP concerns). Licensing server communication encrypted. Industry-standard (Dassault Systèmes) security practices. No compliance certifications mentioned.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Abaqus software stability. License server availability critical. Simulation jobs are long-running and async. No SLA as MCP wrapper. Version compatibility with Abaqus release cycles. Suitable for engineering automation but not real-time operations.

af_score_components.notes: Abell — appears to be a static site generator or framework (details sparse). No MCP server. Documentation minimal. No authentication documented. Rate limits unclear. Very minimal information available. Possibly deprecated or low-activity project.

security_score_components.security_notes: No information on security posture. Likely low-risk for static site generation. No obvious auth requirement. No compliance information. Community project — security depends on maintainer diligence.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Low-activity project based on available information. No SLA. Build-time tool (not runtime critical). Reliability depends on framework stability. Limited community support evident.

af_score_components.notes: ABI-to-MCP — converts blockchain ABIs to MCP servers. Has MCP (quality 65). Documentation minimal. Blockchain-focused tool. No built-in auth (stateless). Rate limits depend on blockchain RPC. Niche use case (Web3 agents).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for RPC calls. Smart contract interactions via on-chain auth. No centralized secret storage (RPC key required). Blockchain calls are immutable/traceable. RPC key exposure is primary risk. Transaction signing security depends on wallet implementation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on blockchain network (Ethereum, etc.) and RPC provider. ABI parsing is deterministic. No SLA. Network congestion affects transaction finality. Suitable for Web3 automation but blockchain confirmation times are inherent constraint.

af_score_components.notes: Ableton Copilot MCP — AI music production assistant. Has MCP (quality 75). Documentation for Ableton context. Wraps Ableton Live API. License required. Music generation is async. Rate limits depend on Ableton stability.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for network communication. Ableton license-based auth. File access (music projects) requires careful handling. Audio file processing could expose artist work. Credential management for Ableton integration. No compliance certifications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Ableton Live stability and availability. Real-time audio processing adds latency considerations. No SLA. Ableton version compatibility critical. Suitable for creative automation but not production music delivery.

af_score_components.notes: Ableton Live MCP server — direct Ableton Live integration. Has MCP (quality 75). Music production focused. Ableton must be running locally. No auth for local operation (local IPC). Rate limits depend on Ableton responsiveness.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local IPC (no network security needed). No auth for local operation. Full access to Ableton project files. User filesystem permissions apply. No encryption for local communication. Suitable only for trusted local development.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Ableton Live stability (version-specific). Local tool — reliability is user-managed. No SLA. Real-time constraints for audio processing. Suitable for local music production automation.

af_score_components.notes: AbraFlexi — likely accounting/business software integration (unclear from name). No MCP server documented. Minimal documentation available. Auth model uncertain. Not enough information for comprehensive evaluation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Insufficient information. Assume business software standards if accounting-focused. Likely has API key or session-based auth. Financial data handling if relevant requires strong controls.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Insufficient information for reliability assessment. Likely business-class reliability if accounting software. No documentation available.

af_score_components.notes: Academia.edu MCP server for academic paper access. Has MCP (quality 70). Academia.edu API integration. Documentation adequate. API key required. Rate limits moderate for academic lookups. Research-focused tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth required. Academia.edu infrastructure (reasonable security). Research metadata public but aggregation creates fingerprinting risk. Author/institution patterns could reveal research interests.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Academia.edu API stability (mature platform). No SLA published. Paper metadata updates can cause API changes. Rate limits per account. Suitable for research automation and paper discovery.

af_score_components.notes: Academic Research MCP server — research paper discovery. Has MCP (quality 70). Likely integrates multiple academic sources. Documentation moderate. Auth varies by source. Rate limits inherited. Scholarly focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for API calls. Multiple source integration (credential management). Academic metadata public but aggregation risks. User research interests revealed through query patterns. No PII by design (academic papers are public).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on multiple academic APIs (Scopus, PubMed, arXiv, etc.). Aggregation adds failure complexity. No SLA. Different sources have different rate limits. Suitable for research automation.

af_score_components.notes: Academic Search MCP server — scholarly paper search. Has MCP (quality 70). API aggregation for multiple databases. Documentation adequate. API keys required for premium sources. Rate limits documented (source-dependent).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Multiple API key management (similar to academic-research-mcp). Query patterns reveal research interests. No PII (public metadata). Credential aggregation risk similar to other research tools.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Similar to academic-research-mcp. Aggregates multiple sources with different SLAs. No unified SLA. Rate limit management complex. Suitable for researcher agents accessing multiple databases.
